raw_consumer_statement:
I am submitting this complaint regarding XXXX XXXX failure to protect me as a consumer in a chargeback case involving a product that was clearly misrepresented by the seller. 

On XX/XX/year>, I purchased hair extensions from a company called XXXX, which advertised a specific texture and color. Upon receiving the item, it was immediately apparent that the product was not as describedboth in quality and appearance. I provided American Express with extensive supporting evidence including : Photographs showing the item received does not match the product description. 
Email correspondence where the seller refused a refund and instead offered a small discount or replacement, even while acknowledging the issue. 

Despite this, Amex denied my dispute solely based on the sellers no return policy, which was not prominently disclosed at the time of sale and was irrelevant to the actual issue : material misrepresentation of the product. 

This sets a deeply troubling precedent. If a seller can simply claim no returns on a product that is defective or falsely advertised, it allows bad actors to exploit cardholders and operate fraudulently with impunity. 

I have now lost {$350.00} for a product that was falsely advertised. I expect American Express to uphold its Cardmember Protection policy and investigate sellers who engage in deceptive practices, especially when consumers provide concrete proof. 

I urge the CFPB to review this matter and ensure that American Express is held accountable for failing to protect cardholders in legitimate disputes involving fraudulent merchants. 

Amex Account Ending In XXXX Reference Number : XXXX Merchant Name : XXXX XXXX XXXX Transaction Amount : {$350.00} Transaction Date : XX/XX/year> XXXX
